commentary: Twelve activities this week versus nine last week, and he just kept going — Balmoral on Friday, Bold and the Beautiful on Sunday, and then right back to Prince Alfred on Monday like he hadn't just run 13.5km the day before. That "recovery" run on Tuesday with an average heart rate of 150 bpm has absolutely no business calling itself a recovery run, and he knows it, which is probably why he put the word in quotation marks. What is genuinely worth noticing is that his two Tuesday swims tell a story across the weeks — last Tuesday he was at 148 bpm for 2000m, this Tuesday he held the same distance at 144 bpm, which means he's getting more comfortable in the water even when his legs are already cooked from a run. The volume this week is a bit chaotic and gloriously so, but if he wants to keep stacking weeks like this, something in the schedule needs to actually be easy — not just labelled that way.
metrics_commentary: Twenty point seven kilometres across just two sessions and over seven and a half thousand metres in the pool — baby, you are not playing around. The VO2 Max sitting at 57.3 is genuinely impressive, that is an athletic number, and you should carry it like you carry everything else: like you already know. The HRV at 51 with a seven-day average of 52 is stable and calm, which means your body is recovered and ready, not white-knuckling its way through the week. The only thing worth watching is that two run sessions is a little thin — not because you owe anyone more miles, but because that stride deserves to be shown off a little more often.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, four sessions hit — the programme said to show up and you showed up, full stop. Seven PRs in a single week is the kind of thing that should make you insufferable at brunch, and you have my full permission to be exactly that: legs pressing 168 kilograms, barbell curls up to 35, a seated row at 84, both dumbbell pressing movements cracking new ceilings on the same week — that's not a fluke, that's a body that's been quietly building and just decided to announce itself all at once. The volume number is the one honest gap here — 4,045 kilograms against the previous week's 24,518 is a significant drop, so the strength is clearly there and sharpening, but the total work done pulled back hard, which is worth watching as the programme builds. All of that said, a week where every major pattern — quad, hinge, back, chest, arms — moved in the right direction is a week worth celebrating, and I am celebrating it loudly, on a table, in a crop top, on your behalf.
